comment.body:
### Issue URL (Annoyance) [https://rutube.ru/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Frutube.ru%2F) ### Comment > https://rutube.ru- На этом сайте, каждые 2 минуты в видео показывают рекламу даже на детских каналах, реклама со звуком на всю громкость! Претензия для администрации сайта оказалась неважной. Невозможно смотреть видео до конца, приходится закрывать вкладку когда начинает орать реклама. Пожалуйста, заблокируйте рекламу в видеороликах ### Screenshots

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 7 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
Both rules are required. Please review the investigation results below and approve them. - The `.hepsiads-voltran-body` rule is necessary for both desktop and mobile. It was added in commit #219101.
Screenshot (Disabled TurkishFilter state)
- The `#DESKTOP_MASTHEAD-googleAdvertisement` rule added at https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/132406#issuecomment-1280742250 still appears on desktop.
Screenshot (Enabled TurkishFilter)
--- By the way, I discovered these initial rule commits using `git log --oneline -S - `. I will use this method to continue searching when a certain rule is added and where it appears, not by guessing or spending more time.

issue.title: Fix #229613

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
I'm on vacation on the French/Monégasque/Italian Riviera and potentially Sweden, so I can't properly look into it before Thursday.

But IIRC, most of those by design of mine, since Google AI search results are far less popular than what many would think, esp. if they are shown on top above normal search results. One look at r/GoogleAiGoneWild would show why.

But screenshot 1 of the recentmost report above is fully valid, as it was not my plan to block the dedicated "Ask AI" page. That one I'll make sure to fix.

comment.body:
I'm the creator of this ticket.
After further testing, I completely disabled the browser’s built-in translation feature (Edge translation) and the purchasing option on the DJI store page now appears correctly. The issue was caused by the browser translation interfering with the page, it has nothing to do with AdGuard. 
We can safely close this ticket. Thank you for your support!

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

Considering that enabling the Other Annoyances filter in AdGuard for Safari does not block Google’s AI Overview, this is almost definitely an issue.

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
This has been reported before in #162965 and was patched with the CSS Fix Rule below. ```adblock ! https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/162965 baidu.com#$#.ec_wise_ad { position: absolute !important; left: -3000px !important; } ```
Sometimes this rule isn't applied, causing problems.
I wanted to understand why @AdamWr used the CSS Fix Rule instead of the Cosmetic rule. I need this answer to decide whether to add a `NOT_OPTIMIZED` comment beforehand.

comment.body:
The upper two rules are correctly included in AdGuard Android, iOS, and other platforms' filters registry.
- https://filters.adtidy.org/android/filters/224_optimized.txt
- https://filters.adtidy.org/ios/filters/224_optimized.txt
- https://filters.adtidy.org/extension/chromium/filters/224_optimized.txt
- etc...

> [!NOTE]
> The language-specific filter URL `224` key is found in [AdguardTeam/FiltersRegistry `filters/filter_224_Chinese/metadata.json#L10`](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/FiltersRegistry/blob/d9f375469cb54cc0b004020f04e2b6ce3507adb1/filters/filter_224_Chinese/metadata.json#L10)

The cause was an intermittent HTTPS filtering failure that could not be fixed with filter updates, so I closed this issue.

issue.title: www.youtube.com

labels.name: T: Incorrect Blocking
labels.description: Website or app not working properly due to filtering

labels.name: T: Anti Adblock Script
labels.description: The site detects ad blocking

labels.name: T: Ads
labels.description: Missed ads

labels.name: T: Social Widget
labels.description: Social widgets, share, like buttons and popups

labels.name: T: Annoyance
labels.description: Elements that annoy users

labels.name: NSFW
labels.description: Nudes and porn sites

labels.name: N: Other
labels.description: Non-AdGuard ad blocker

labels.name: A: Problematic
labels.description: Issues, which cannot be resolved easily

issue.body:
# ATTENTION

> [!NOTE]  
> YouTube changes their detection scripts regularly. That means that sometime you can face the detection message.
> If all the following instructions do not work, please **email filters@adguard.com** or wait until any update is available. 

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use another adblocker in the same time because using another adblocker can delay/lags the YouTube loading (e.g., AdBlock Plus and AdBlock)[^1] OR interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use an userscript related to the bypassing YouTube detection message like [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing).
> Recently, YouTube anti-adblock script added infinite dummy advertisements to try to neutralize the userscript. ( [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing#264](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing/issues/264) )

> [!WARNING]
> Please check that there no another web browser extension that has adblocking feature is enabled on your web browser. It can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!IMPORTANT]  
> Saving memory feature of your web browser or sleep feature of your operating system can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.
> In this case, please re-enable AdGuard's filtering by toggling the protection toggle.
> Following the instructions can be needed.

### The instructions
1. Force to update the filters by clicking the reload button in AdGuard Browser Extension/AdGuard mobile or update tray menu in your system tray. [^2]
2. Reload the YouTube webpage without cache by pressing <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd> [^3].
3. Clear cache, cookies and site data of your web browser.
4. Re-open your web browser completely (checking your system monitor[^4] will be better).

[^1]: https://gitlab.com/adblockinc/ext/adblockplus/adblockplusui/-/issues/1576
[^2]: [AdGuard for Windows](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-windows/overview/#tray-menu); [AdGuard for Mac](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-mac/overview/#updates-check); 
[^3]: On macOS, <kbd>Command</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd>
[^4]: Task Manager on Windows, System Monitor on GNOME Shell, KDE System Guard on KDE Plasma or Activity Monitor on macOS.

issue.title: False positive: tracker.metricool.com — first-party SaaS application

labels.name: A: In progress
labels.description: Work on the issue is in progress

issue.body:
Hello,

I'm a Senior Support Engineer at Metricool (https://metricool.com), a social media management platform with over 5 million users.

Metricool domains are currently included in your blocklists, which causes our web application to fail entirely for users with DNS-level blocking enabled. This is especially critical for our White Label Agencies product, where clients access our platform under their own custom domains — the block completely breaks their service.

## Why this is a false positive

Metricool is a first-party SaaS application, not an ad network or cross-site tracker. Users actively sign up, log in, and use the product to manage their social media. Our domains serve the application itself — blocking them is equivalent to blocking the product entirely.

The static.metricool.com/metricool.com domain serves both application assets and an opt-in website analytics pixel that site owners voluntarily install. It does not perform cross-site tracking, fingerprinting, or serve ads.

Would appreciate a review and removal from the affected lists. Happy to answer any questions.

Thank you.

Luca Di Paola
Senior Support Engineer — Metricool

comment.body:
> One look at r/GoogleAiGoneWild would show why.

I use the AI overview daily, so it's definitely not universally hated. Judging it by a glitch subreddit is like saying autocorrect is useless just because of typo meme compilations. 
Would be great to just drop this specific block, as most people don't expect a general "annoyances" toggle to secretly disable a core search engine feature.

I report these a couple of times a week now. This particular site presents two pop up ads, and an advertisement video. One can only conclude that AdGuard simply doesn't work anymore.... and I have paid my license fee for another year in exchange for zip. The only thing that makes this worse is (i) the systematic low or medium priority designation, and (ii) the regular response I get from AdGuard tech indicating to me "cannot recreate", together with some suggested action which either has already been performed, or makes no sense. ``` Username: @rockettnyc ``` ### Screenshots

comment.body:
What's wrong? The website loads fine on my end.

Please try following actions, then check the problem again:
1. Disable all content blockers in Safari settings and enable them again
2. Restart Safari
3. Restart AdGuard
4. Check for filters update in AdGuard.

### Important:
<!-- **AdGuard for iOS** -->
* Do not close AdGuard window ~60 seconds, because iOS will break configuration process.
<!-- **AdGuard for Safari** -->
* The filters update process is fully completed ~10 seconds after the manual filter update check is completed.

This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Ads) [https://www.entrepreneur.com/starting-a-business/eric-ries-on-4-common-misconceptions-about-lean-startup/286701](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.entrepreneur.com%2Fstarting-a-business%2Feric-ries-on-4-common-misconceptions-about-lean-startup%2F286701) ### Comment > Problem description: > When using AdGuard AdBlocker in Google Chrome, manually blocking elements on a webpage (via the “Block this element” feature) can trigger a persistent “Rule limits” warning stating that filter rule limits have been exceeded. In this case, the warning appeared after attempting to block UI elements such as a “Subscribe now” button and an email newsletter banner on an article. > > After the warning appears, Chrome reports that it has modified the list of active built-in filters due to rule limits, reducing the enabled filter set. The warning then persists even after attempting to restore or adjust filter configurations. Even after substantially lowering the number of active filters below the previously working configuration, the warning still continues to appear. > > Steps to reproduce the problem: > > Install AdGuard AdBlocker in Google Chrome (Manifest V3 environment). > Open a news/article website with subscription prompts (e.g. “Subscribe now” button and email newsletter banner). > > Use AdGuard’s “Block this element” tool on multiple page elements. > > Trigger filter update check (filters confirmed up to date). > > Continue browsing or refresh the page. > > AdGuard displays: “Your browser has modified the list of active built-in filters – Rule limits.” > > Built-in filter set is reduced by Chrome/AdGuard. > > Attempt to restore filters previously active before the warning. > > Attempt to resolve by significantly reducing the number of enabled built-in filters. > > See that warning persists despite reduced filter load. > > Environment: > > Browser: Google Chrome (Manifest V3) > Extension: AdGuard AdBlocker (latest version) > OS: Windows 10/11 desktop environment > > Notes: > The issue appears to be linked to Chrome’s MV3 rule quota enforcement interacting with AdGuard’s built-in filter system. Once the warning state is triggered, it persists even after filter reactivation attempts and substantial reduction of enabled filters, suggesting the configuration does not properly recalculate or recover from the rule limit state. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/kry3j4jw4kggsg80kws08c4s8gws4s02061pt1bogi.png?nc=1)
Screenshot 2 ![Screenshot 2](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/g52fca3zca8sw8og4048wgkw0444osgle0zpv3sipc.png?nc=1)

AdGuard Base filter

AdGuard Cookie Notices filter,
AdGuard Popups filter,
AdGuard Other Annoyances filter

comment.body:
You are right - we can't redefine Chrome's mv3 limits.
You may try mv2 - it still works


1. https://www.autocontrol.app/force-enable-Manifest-V2-support
    - Download ***Force Manifest V2 availability.reg*** to your computer.
    - Double-click on the file and confirm the message box.
    
    This action could be reverted by ***Remove Manifest V2 availability.reg*** + steps ***2***, ***3***.
    
2. Go to `chrome://policy` and click on the ***Reload** policies* button.
3. Go to `chrome://flags/`
    - Switch ***Allow legacy extension manifest versions*** to ***Enabled***
    - Maybe ***Temporarily unexpire * flags*** also required.
    - Restart the browser.
4. Run browser with shortcut parameter (add to ***Target*** field in details)
`-disable-features=ExtensionManifestV2Unsupported,ExtensionManifestV2Disabled` 
It seems not required in Chrome 138, but 140 (dev/canary) does not allow enabling mv2 extensions without this command.
 
https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/adguard-adblocker-mv2/gfggjaccafhcbfogfkogggoepomehbjl

issue.title: False positive: tracker.metricool.com — first-party SaaS application

labels.name: A: In progress
labels.description: Work on the issue is in progress

issue.body:
Hello,

I'm a Senior Support Engineer at Metricool (https://metricool.com), a social media management platform with over 5 million users.

Metricool domains are currently included in your blocklists, which causes our web application to fail entirely for users with DNS-level blocking enabled. This is especially critical for our White Label Agencies product, where clients access our platform under their own custom domains — the block completely breaks their service.

## Why this is a false positive

Metricool is a first-party SaaS application, not an ad network or cross-site tracker. Users actively sign up, log in, and use the product to manage their social media. Our domains serve the application itself — blocking them is equivalent to blocking the product entirely.

The static.metricool.com/metricool.com domain serves both application assets and an opt-in website analytics pixel that site owners voluntarily install. It does not perform cross-site tracking, fingerprinting, or serve ads.

Would appreciate a review and removal from the affected lists. Happy to answer any questions.

Thank you.

Luca Di Paola
Senior Support Engineer — Metricool

### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://vk.com/audio-2001689051_148689051](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fvk.com%2Faudio-2001689051_148689051) ### Comment > Выключается плеер вконтакте когда включен Adguard. Когда выключен - появляется. Музыка играет по меню сверху или при нажатии кнопки "слушать". Вероятно из за того что гении из команды ВК что то изменили в коде виджета плеера и там по умолчанию должна крутиться реклама. > > The VKontakte player turns off when Adguard is turned on. When it's turned off, it appears. Music plays from the menu above or when you press the "listen" button.
